app.name: JSignPdf
app.one_liner: A cross-platform Java desktop application for adding digital signatures to PDF documents with support for hardware tokens, RFC 3161 timestamping, and long-term validation.

screens.name: Main Signing Interface
screens.purpose: Allows users to load PDFs, preview documents, drag-to-place visible signatures, configure signing options, and initiate the signing process.
primary_actions: Load PDF via drag-and-drop or file picker
Place visible signature by dragging onto preview

rust_commands.name: sign_pdf_cli
rust_commands.purpose: Sign one or more PDFs non-interactively using command-line parameters with full parity to GUI capabilities.

mvp_plan: week 1: Implement JavaFX PDF preview with drag-to-place signature overlay
week 1: Integrate PKCS#12 and PKCS#11 keystore selection
week 1: Support OCSP/CRL embedding toggle for LTV
acceptance_tests: CLI mode signs 100 PDFs in batch using preconfigured properties without GUI interaction.
User can load an encrypted PDF, place a visible signature via drag, and successfully sign with a PKCS#11 hardware token.
